file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive reference documentation for the advanced example module
key_sections: Quick start guide with code examples
Key patterns: multi-tenant isolation, authorization, validation, events, hooks, soft delete
file_insights.documentation_quality: Excellent - includes patterns, examples, checklists, and references to other documentation

file_insights.purpose: HTTP request handlers with authorization and tenant context
key_patterns: Extends BaseController for consistent response formatting
RESTful CRUD operations (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
Query parameter handling with type-safe query objects
Consistent error handling through service layer
responsibilities: List examples with pagination and filtering
Get single examples by ID or slug

file_insights.purpose: Business logic orchestration and domain operations
responsibilities: Event emission for important state changes

file_insights.purpose: Data access layer with tenant isolation
key_patterns: Every query includes organizationId for multi-tenancy

file_insights.purpose: Zod validation schemas for all inputs

exports: Request types: Create, Update, ChangeStatus, Process

file_insights.pattern: Each event has typed payload interface

file_insights.purpose: Extensibility hook system for behavioral customization
Route configuration and path builder utilities
content: Path builder functions for type-safe URL generation

file_insights.purpose: Module manifest with metadata and configuration
Single public API entry point for module exports
file_insights.principle: Barrel export for clean external imports

patterns_discovered.description: Every database query includes organizationId to ensure data isolation between tenants
patterns_discovered.implementation: Repository methods require organizationId parameter; schema validations enforce it
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents cross-organization data leaks; enforces tenant boundaries at database layer

patterns_discovered.description: @Authorized decorator on controller endpoints checks user permissions before execution
patterns_discovered.implementation: @Authorized(['example:read']) on get endpoints, specific permissions per operation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Fine-grained access control; declarative security at endpoint level

patterns_discovered.description: All input validation uses Zod schemas for type-safe runtime validation
patterns_discovered.implementation: Dedicated schema.ts file; schemas used in service layer before operations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type safety; consistent validation; schema reuse; error handling

patterns_discovered.description: Modules emit events for important state changes; other modules subscribe to events
patterns_discovered.implementation: Events defined in events.ts; emitted from service layer; listened in module hooks
patterns_discovered.benefit: Loose coupling; extensibility; audit trail; cross-module integration

patterns_discovered.description: Before/after hooks at key operations allow behavioral customization without code changes
patterns_discovered.implementation: Hooks defined in hooks.ts; executed from service layer with context
patterns_discovered.benefit: Non-invasive extensibility; plugin architecture; business logic customization

patterns_discovered.description: Data is never physically deleted; deletedAt field marks as deleted, included in queries
patterns_discovered.implementation: Repository includes soft delete checks in all list queries; softDelete() sets deletedAt
patterns_discovered.benefit: Data recovery; audit compliance; referential integrity preservation

patterns_discovered.description: Centralized data access layer isolates business logic from database implementation
patterns_discovered.implementation: Repository class wraps Prisma calls; service calls repository methods
patterns_discovered.benefit: Testability; database agnosticism; consistent query patterns

patterns_discovered.description: Service handles business logic, validation, hook execution, and event emission
patterns_discovered.implementation: Service validates input; executes hooks; delegates to repository; emits events
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single responsibility; testable business logic; consistent error handling

patterns_discovered.description: Controllers extend BaseController with consistent response formatting methods
patterns_discovered.implementation: success(), paginated(), error() methods; standardized API response format
patterns_discovered.benefit: Consistent API responses; reduced boilerplate; easier client consumption

patterns_discovered.description: Services and controllers use @injectable and @inject for loose coupling
patterns_discovered.implementation: Service registered in DI container; injected into controller and other services

patterns_discovered.description: Single index.ts barrel export for all public APIs
patterns_discovered.implementation: Module, Service, Types, Enums all exported from index.ts
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clean external imports; controls public API surface; implementation hiding

patterns_discovered.description: Module implements load, enable, disable, unload methods for lifecycle management
patterns_discovered.implementation: Module class with lifecycle methods; called by framework during module management
patterns_discovered.benefit: Graceful initialization and cleanup; proper resource management; dependency ordering

patterns_discovered.description: Separate types for requests, responses, and internal domain models
patterns_discovered.benefit: API contracts decoupled from internal models; evolution flexibility

patterns_discovered.description: List operations support pagination, sorting, and filtering parameters
patterns_discovered.implementation: ListExamplesQuery type; repository pagination methods; service filtering logic
patterns_discovered.benefit: Scalable list operations; UI flexibility; database optimization

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: organizationId must be included in EVERY database query
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Repository pattern enforces this; service layer validates it
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Repository methods require organizationId parameter; schema validation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Soft delete queries must filter out deletedAt records
gotchas_discovered.context: Deleted items appear in lists if notDeleted filter forgotten
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Use predefined notDeleted const in all list queries
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Consistent pattern in repository; query builder helper

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: @Authorized decorator must be present on all public endpoints
gotchas_discovered.context: Unauthenticated access to protected endpoints if omitted
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Type-safe permissions in module.json; lint rules could enforce

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Input validation must happen before business logic execution
gotchas_discovered.context: Invalid data can corrupt database or trigger unexpected behavior
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Service.create() validates input first; then executes hooks
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Zod schemas in dedicated file; validation early in service methods

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Hooks execute with potentially modified context data
gotchas_discovered.context: Hooks can change input data; downstream code must handle modifications
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Hook results are returned and used; document mutation expectations
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Clear hook documentation; type safety for hook context

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Events are emitted asynchronously; side effects not guaranteed in order
gotchas_discovered.context: Event listeners may execute out of order; dependent operations may race
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Event emissions are fire-and-forget; don't depend on event order
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Document event ordering guarantees; use transactions for critical operations

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Pagination requires both limit and offset; defaults matter
gotchas_discovered.context: Unexpected pagination behavior if defaults not applied
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Query parameters have sensible defaults; document them
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Default values in schema; consistent pagination across all list operations

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Slug uniqueness must be scoped to organization
gotchas_discovered.context: Slug conflicts if not checking organizationId
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Repository slug check includes organizationId; type system documents it

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Error handling differences between controller and service layers
gotchas_discovered.context: AppException thrown in service; controller converts to HTTP response
gotchas_discovered.lesson: Service throws exceptions; controller handles them; consistent error responses
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Use AppException consistently; error handler middleware catches all

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Type exports must not leak internal implementation details
gotchas_discovered.context: Prisma types or internal structures exposed through index.ts
gotchas_discovered.lesson: index.ts only exports public types and main classes
gotchas_discovered.prevention: Separate internal and public type definitions; barrel export discipline

approach_outcome.scope: Complete module implementation with all E-Menum patterns
key_accomplishments: Comprehensive README.md with patterns, examples, and best practices guide
Full-featured controller with all CRUD and advanced operations
Service layer with hooks, events, and validation integration
Repository pattern with multi-tenant isolation and soft delete
Zod validation schemas for all inputs
Complete TypeScript type definitions and enums
quality_indicators: Extensive JSDoc comments in every file
approach_outcome.reference_value: High - This module is intended as a template for future module development
approach_outcome.documentation_completeness: Excellent - README covers all patterns, endpoints, events, hooks, and usage
approach_outcome.pattern_coverage: All major E-Menum patterns demonstrated: multi-tenancy, auth, validation, events, hooks, soft delete, DI, lifecycle management

recommendations.recommendation: Use this module as the template for ALL future module development
recommendations.rationale: Demonstrates all patterns and best practices; ensures consistency across codebase
recommendations.implementation: Reference README instructions when creating new modules; copy structure

recommendations.recommendation: Consider creating a module scaffolding tool that generates boilerplate from this template
recommendations.rationale: New modules could be generated with correct structure automatically
recommendations.impact: Reduces manual work; ensures pattern compliance; accelerates development

recommendations.recommendation: Link all module development documentation to this reference implementation
recommendations.rationale: Single source of truth for module patterns; reduces documentation scatter

recommendations.recommendation: Create corresponding test examples demonstrating unit and integration testing patterns
recommendations.rationale: Module includes no tests; developers need examples of testing patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Implement linting rules to enforce missing @Authorized decorators and organizationId parameters
recommendations.rationale: Most common mistakes are authorization and multi-tenant isolation bugs
recommendations.implementation: ESLint rules or pre-commit hooks to validate these patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Add stricter TypeScript settings to catch missing organizationId parameters
recommendations.rationale: Multi-tenant isolation is critical; type system should enforce it
recommendations.suggestion: Brand type for organizationId to prevent string confusion
Add permission naming guidelines to module development guide

recommendations.recommendation: Create specific AppException subclasses for domain errors (NotFoundError, ValidationError, etc.)
recommendations.rationale: Allows more granular error handling; better error codes for clients
recommendations.current_pattern: Generic AppException; could be specialized per module

recommendations.recommendation: Consider permanent deletion for specific entities if GDPR/compliance requires true deletion
recommendations.rationale: Current pattern always uses soft delete; some data may need permanent removal
recommendations.suggestion: Document when to use soft vs hard delete

recommendations.recommendation: Document hook execution order and priority system in hook system documentation
recommendations.rationale: Hooks have priorities; need clear documentation of execution semantics
recommendations.suggestion: Add hook priority examples and guidelines to this README
